title: smoke-hot slovakian oranges confit
description: A bitter, dense, and comforting Slovakian dish featuring smoky oranges, perfect for a sick day. This recipe is FODMAP-friendly and uses ingredients that are easily digestible.
instructions: In a small saucepan, combine the smoked paprika, orange marmalade, honey, salt, and black pepper. Heat over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the honey has dissolved.
Add the freshly squeezed orange juice to the saucepan and stir to combine.
Transfer the mixture to a 9x13 inch baking dish and drizzle with olive oil.
Arrange the orange slices on top of the mixture, leaving a 1 inch border around the edges.
Cover the dish with aluminum foil and bake for 1 hour.
Remove the foil and continue baking for an additional 30 minutes, or until the oranges are tender and caramelized.
Remove the dish from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Use a slotted spoon to transfer the oranges to a serving dish, leaving the juices behind.
Garnish with fresh parsley, if desired.
Serve warm or at room temperature.
